channel.title: World
channel.description: The Washington Post World section provides information and analysis of breaking world news stories. In addition to our world news and video, Post World News offers discussions and blogs on major international news and economic issues.

item.title: NATO allies grapple with U.S. pullback, confusion over its military plans
item.dc:creator: Ellen Francis
item.description: As Secretary of State Marco Rubio meets with NATO foreign ministers in Sweden, the focus is on the U.S. moving to reduce its military footprint in Europe.

item.title: Police urge new witnesses to speak out in Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor inquiry
item.dc:creator: Victoria Craw
item.description: The appeal marked the first significant update since the former British prince was arrested in February over allegations of misconduct while in office.

item.title: U.S. bears brunt of Israel’s missile defense, Pentagon assessments show
item.dc:creator: John Hudson
item.description: Amid hostilities with Iran, the American military expended far more advanced interceptors to protect Israel than Israeli forces did, according to Defense Department data.

item.title: In rare clash, North Korea defeats South Korea … on the soccer field
item.dc:creator: Michelle Lee
item.description: In an inter-peninsula match-up between women's teams that showed the limits of sports diplomacy, players from North Korea remained stone-faced — until they won.

item.title: Ebola responders say aid cuts by Western nations left them ill-equipped for outbreak
item.dc:creator: Rael Ombuor, Rachel Chason, Lauren Weber, Lena H. Sun
item.description: The sweeping aid cuts have left the Democratic Republic of Congo struggling to contain an Ebola outbreak despite its extensive experience with the disease.

item.title: As U.S. war redraws Middle East, Turkey braces for rivalry with Israel
item.dc:creator: Ellen Francis, Burhan Yüksekkaș
item.description: Amid the unpredictability of President Trump's Middle East policies, Turkey is calling for “regional ownership” and pursuing new security pacts.
